title: Guidance Clarity Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill makes all government agencies add a warning message to the first page of their guidance documents. The warning must tell people that the guidance is not a real law and cannot be enforced like a law. It also must explain that the guidance is only meant to help people better understand rules that already exist. This way, people will know that guidance documents are just helpful explanations, not new laws they have to follow.
last_action_text: Placed on Senate Legislative Calendar under General Orders. Calendar No. 250.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Guidance Clarity Act of two thousand twenty-five.

sections.title: Section 2 Guidance Clarity Statement Required
sections.text: Starting thirty days after the Office of Management and Budget creates instructions for this law, all government agencies must add a special statement to their guidance documents. This statement must be placed clearly on the first page of any guidance document the agency creates. The statement must say exactly this: The contents of this document do not have the force and effect of law and do not, of themselves, bind the public or the agency. This document is intended only to provide clarity to the public regarding existing requirements under the law or agency policies. Within ninety days after this law is passed, the Director of the Office of Management and Budget must create instructions to help agencies follow this law.
